AIAAIC Repository · Conversational AI harm analysis

Chatbot Harm Atlas

An interactive view of how chatbot and conversational-AI incidents are coded as harm: bodily, psychological, financial, reputational, civic, privacy-related, and environmental. Each count is a harm mention, not necessarily a unique incident: a single incident may contain multiple harms.

Method. The default view filters the repository to likely chatbot or conversational-AI cases using system names and incident text: ChatGPT, chatbot, assistant, Gemini, Claude, Copilot, Character.AI, Alexa, Siri, GPT, LLM, companion, and similar terms. Switch to “All AI incidents” to compare against the full repository.

Life domains

Interpretive note

The taxonomy is useful because it shows that chatbot harm is not one thing. It clusters around emotional distress, privacy loss, financial loss, reputational damage, institutional trust, and — in a smaller number of cases — death, injury, and environmental burden.

Most frequent harm types

Harm mentions over time

Select a harm type

Source: uploaded file “AIAAIC Repository - Incidents.csv.” Categories shown here derive from the repository’s external-harm taxonomy, lightly normalized for spelling variants and grouped into interpretive “life domains” for presentation. The chatbot subset is heuristic and should be treated as a working analytical cut, not an official AIAAIC category.
